signals.name: Michael Burry
signals.latest_title: MEGA Trading Post/Short Thoughts Mash-Up June 2, 2026

signals.connection: Directly engages with the SpaceX S-1 story; his ongoing AI bubble skepticism (short Nvidia/Palantir thesis) is relevant to Anthropic's $965B IPO valuation and AI market exuberance.

signals.name: Ben Thompson
signals.latest_title: An Interview with Ben Thompson at the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference
signals.summary: Published June 1 and active through June 4; covered the implications of the compute shortage on Aggregation Theory and consumer AI, SpaceX hype and the 'Elon Bargain,' Nvidia and the Neoclouds, and data center discontent. Also published 'The Google Capital Company' analyzing Google's AI capex strategy and equity issuance.
signals.connection: Directly covers the SpaceX S-1, AI data center buildout dynamics, and the capital structures of hyperscalers — all core to today's top stories on IPOs, grid crisis, and AI infrastructure.

signals.name: Benedict Evans
signals.summary: Updated his semi-annual macro tech presentation 'AI eats the world' in May 2026; also recently gave a podcast interview with Lenny Rachitsky (June 1) where he framed AI as a 1997-internet-scale shift — significant but with most applications still unbuilt. Posted on Threads that the lack of a good theory for why LLMs work is not proof they have no limits.
signals.connection: His 'AI eats the world' presentation and '1997 moment' framing directly contextualize the Anthropic IPO, frontier AI governance debates, and the data center buildout cycle.

signals.name: Lenny Rachitsky
signals.latest_title: A rational conversation on where AI is actually going with Benedict Evans
signals.summary: Promoted and shared key takeaways from his June 1 podcast with Benedict Evans, summarizing Evans' view that 'we're in 1997 for AI' — a big-deal shift where most of what will be built hasn't been built yet. Active on X promoting the episode.
signals.connection: Directly connects to Anthropic IPO, frontier AI governance, and the broader question of where AI is in its adoption curve — relevant to labor, applied tech, and AI regulation topics.

signals.name: Andrej Karpathy
signals.summary: Announced on May 19 that he joined Anthropic's pretraining team to lead a group using Claude to accelerate pretraining research — the post drew ~3 million views in an hour. He joins as Anthropic files a confidential IPO at a ~$1T valuation.
signals.connection: His Anthropic hire is directly tied to today's top story on the Anthropic IPO; his 'autoresearch' and 'Karpathy Loop' work on AI-accelerated pretraining connects to frontier tech and AI capability advancement.
